sourceTitle: This project management system connects the dots for your product team

questions.question: According to the guide, which of the following is NOT listed as a common problem that product teams face with traditional project management tools?

options.text: Dispersed, inaccessible information across multiple tools
options.explanation: The guide explicitly mentions scattered information as a problem.

options.text: Lack of transparency for the team and the broader organization
options.explanation: Transparency issues are highlighted as a key pain point.

options.text: Rigid systems that force teams to conform to the software's workflow
options.explanation: The guide calls out rigidity as a flaw of many existing tools.

options.text: Insufficient color‑coding options for task priority
options.explanation: Color‑coding is not mentioned; the guide focuses on information silos, transparency, and rigidity.

questions.question: When creating the initial board for project tracking, which Notion command is used to insert the board on a new page?

options.text: /table
options.explanation: /table creates a table view, not a board.

options.text: /board
options.explanation: Typing /board and selecting "Board – Full page" creates the kanban‑style board.

options.text: /calendar
options.explanation: /calendar creates a calendar view, which is not the board step described.

options.text: /gallery
options.explanation: /gallery creates a gallery view, not the board used for project stages.

questions.question: Which feature allows you to see selected properties directly on the cards in a board view, reducing the need to open each card?

options.text: Group By
options.explanation: Group By changes how cards are clustered, not which properties are displayed.

options.text: Properties toggle in the view settings
options.explanation: Turning on properties in the view settings shows chosen fields on each card.

options.text: Sort
options.explanation: Sorting orders cards but does not affect visible properties.

options.text: Relation property
options.explanation: Relations link databases but do not control on‑card property visibility.

questions.question: How can you create a view that only shows projects launching this week?

options.text: Add a filter on the launch date property to "is on or after" today and "is before" 7 days from now
options.explanation: Filtering by date range isolates projects launching within the next week.

options.text: Rename the board columns to "This Week" and "Next Week"
options.explanation: Renaming columns does not filter the dataset.

options.text: Create a new property called "This Week" and manually check it for each project
options.explanation: Manual tagging works but is not the recommended filter‑based method.

options.text: Use the Group By feature on the "Status" property
options.explanation: Grouping by status does not limit results to a specific time frame.

questions.question: What is the primary benefit of using a Relation property between the project board and a separate Tasks database?

options.text: It automatically assigns tasks to the correct team members
options.explanation: Relation links records; assignment still requires a People property.

options.text: It lets you view and edit related tasks directly from the project card
options.explanation: Relation surfaces linked items, giving context without leaving the card.

options.text: It changes the board layout from columns to rows
options.explanation: Layout is controlled by view type, not by relations.

options.text: It converts tasks into a calendar view automatically
options.explanation: Relations do not change view types; you would need to create a calendar view separately.

questions.question: Which of the following template components is specifically recommended for a "Project spec" template?

options.text: A Select property for "Feature Request" tags
options.explanation: Feature request tags belong to a RFC template, not the spec.

options.text: H2 header "Dependencies & Risks" with H3 sub‑headers for "Stakeholders" and "Risks"
options.explanation: The guide lists this exact structure for the spec template.

options.text: A Code block for embedding API endpoints
options.explanation: Code blocks are useful but not highlighted as a core spec section.

options.text: An embedded Figma prototype at the top of the page
options.explanation: Embedding Figma is suggested for general pages, not a required spec component.

questions.question: What is the recommended way to surface a view of the project board on the product team's wiki page?

options.text: Copy‑paste the board URL into the wiki page
options.explanation: A raw URL does not embed a live view.

options.text: Use the /link command to create a linked database, then add a filtered view
options.explanation: Creating a linked database and adding a view is the method described.

options.text: Export the board as a PDF and upload it
options.explanation: PDFs are static and would not stay up‑to‑date.

options.text: Add the board as an embed block using an iFrame
options.explanation: Notion does not support iFrame embeds for its own pages; linked databases are the proper approach.

questions.question: Why does the guide advise against using rigid, one‑size‑fits‑all project management software?

options.text: Because rigid tools are usually more expensive than Notion
options.explanation: Cost is not the primary concern highlighted.

options.text: Because they force teams to adapt their processes to the tool instead of the tool adapting to the team's workflow
options.explanation: The guide emphasizes molding software to the team's needs, not the reverse.

options.text: Because they lack any kind of reporting capability
options.explanation: Reporting may be limited, but the guide focuses on flexibility, not reporting.

options.text: Because they cannot be integrated with external services like Figma or Typeform
options.explanation: Integration is a concern, yet the core argument is about flexibility of process.

questions.question: Which Notion feature enables real‑time collaboration by allowing you to tag teammates in comments?

options.text: The @ symbol
options.explanation: Using @ followed by a name creates a mention in comments.

options.text: The # symbol
options.explanation: # creates a link to another page, not a mention.

options.text: The /embed command
options.explanation: /embed inserts external content, not mentions.

options.text: The *bold* formatting shortcut
options.explanation: Bold formatting does not involve tagging.

questions.question: What is the purpose of adding a "Feedback" tag to feature requests in the customer support database, according to the best practices section?

options.text: To automatically close the request after three days
options.explanation: Tags do not trigger automated closure.

options.text: To prioritize features by surfacing the most requested items to the roadmap via a Relation property
options.explanation: Feedback tags help surface high‑demand features for roadmap decisions.

options.text: To assign a dedicated developer to each request
options.explanation: Assignment requires a People property, not just a tag.

options.text: To generate a monthly PDF report of all feedback
options.explanation: Tagging alone does not produce reports; a view or export would be needed.
